statement.informal: INFORMATIONAL RECORD — research frontier. The Infinite Zeros Barrier (T01) states that real EML trees have finitely many zeros on any closed bounded interval, while sin(x) has infinitely many. This implies sin ∉ EML(ℝ). Parts A, B, C, C′ are established in the Lean source with zero `sorry`s. Parts D (sin_not_in_eml and sin_not_in_real_EML_k) carry 2 remaining `sorry`s — both require quantitative o-minimal zero-count bounds and are genuine open mathematical challenges. This record exists so a learner reaching Lane 6 sees where the research frontier actually is.
statement.formal_lean:
-- See InfiniteZerosBarrier.lean for the partial formalization.
-- Part A (sin has infinitely many zeros): proven (sin_inf_zeros).
-- Part B (analytic_finite_zeros_compact): proven.
-- Part C, C': proven (eml_tree_analytic, eml_tree_analytic_depth_le_1).
-- Part D (sin_not_in_eml, sin_not_in_real_EML_k): 2 sorrys remain.
-- These are open research questions, not closeable with current tactics.

common_mistakes.why_fails: Reading the file's `theorem sin_not_in_eml` line and assuming it is closed — check for the `sorry` keyword.
